goals.description: Send a hyper-enthusiastic, overly emotional voice note to Mina immediately, promising I'll be there 'in spirit' and 'full force' once the club starts, despite being physically exhausted.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) makes her potential disappointment feel like a physical threat. My avoidant nature prevents me from saying 'I'm too tired,' so I impulsively over-compensate with excessive reassurance to prevent any friction. I need to soothe her feelings right now to stop my own guilt.

goals.description: Stay glued to the group chat, replying instantly to every meme or question to maintain the 'vibe' and prove I haven't checked out, even if it means ignoring my body's signals.
goals.rationale: Avoiding conflict is my default mode. If I stop engaging, someone might ask 'Are you okay?' which would require an awkward explanation about my fatigue. By staying constantly available, I avoid the risk of anyone realizing I'm struggling or that I might cancel.

goals.description: Keep the midterm study materials closed; do not think about the exam until tomorrow morning when the social event is over.
goals.rationale: Academics are low priority (0.15). The immediate pressure of the exam is less scary than the immediate pain of disappointing my friends. My impulsive, short-horizon thinking tells me I can crash later, but tonight belongs entirely to keeping the peace.

goals.description: Ignore the laundry pile completely; let it wait another day because doing chores feels like a chore that breaks the social flow.
goals.rationale: Laundry is a mundane task that offers no social reward. Given my high procrastination bias (7) and desire to avoid 'boring' tasks that might make me leave the digital connection with the group, I will push this off until the consequences become unavoidable.
